reasoning: The external_body `always_unfold` bridges the intensional gap between the `forall` quantifier embedded inside `always`'s `TempPred::new` closure and a bare `forall` in the ensures. This is an unverified trust assumption that the SMT solver cannot check through the closure boundary.

Both sides reduce to `(ex.nat_to_state)(i + 1)` after unfolding: `ex.suffix(i).head_next()` = `(|j| f(j+i))(1)` = `f(1+i)` and `ex.suffix(i+1).head()` = `(|j| f(j+(i+1)))(0)` = `f(0+(i+1))` = `f(i+1)`. The SMT solver resolves this arithmetic equality directly — this is a desirable and expected property.

A constant execution satisfying `always(lift_action(|s1,s2| s1==v && s2==v))` is correct and expected — the action predicate genuinely holds at every step. This is standard TLA behavior; liveness concerns are orthogonal to safety/action specifications.
